title: America’s Next Top Paddy’s Billboard Model Contest

jokes.text: Who spent $500 for laser hair removal? Right over here, slick. Don't want to have hair down there. You know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Frank questioning absurd expenses on the company card
jokes.punchline: Mac casually admitting to genital hair removal as a business expense
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Mac's shameless justification of personal grooming as business expense shows his narcissism and delusion

jokes.text: Who spent $5,000 for a samurai sword? Your head of security. Yeah. Just wait till he saves your life one day with it.
jokes.setup: Another ridiculous expense being questioned
jokes.punchline: Mac calling himself 'head of security' and defending a samurai sword purchase
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from the hair removal - Mac's delusions of grandeur about being security with a medieval weapon

jokes.text: I'm going to put them on YouTube. That way, I can get discovered by, like, a casting director or a producer, get some kind of a TV development deal.
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining his $6,000 camcorder purchase
jokes.punchline: His delusional plan to get discovered through terrible homemade videos
jokes.explanation: Charlie's naive optimism about YouTube fame perfectly captures his childlike delusion

jokes.text: we write on the billboard 'We have two-for-one drink specials on Thursdays.' And, on the bottom, you have a hilarious picture of Green Man kicking someone in the nuts.
jokes.setup: Charlie presenting what he thinks is a brilliant billboard idea
jokes.punchline: Combining mundane drink specials with random violence from his alter ego
jokes.explanation: Perfectly captures Charlie's bizarre marketing mind - mixing practical info with absurd violence

jokes.text: It is really astonishing how bad your idea is. Go sit down. The adults are working.
jokes.setup: Charlie's enthusiastic billboard pitch
jokes.punchline: Dennis's condescending dismissal treating Charlie like a child
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic cruelty delivered with perfect condescension

jokes.text: It's Advertising 101: long legs, taut breasts and tight poopers.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining his billboard philosophy
jokes.punchline: Clinical description of body parts ending with the childish 'poopers'
jokes.explanation: Frank's crude business wisdom with unexpected childish vocabulary makes it quotable

jokes.text: I would volunteer to be the girl on the billboard, and then you guys would talk about how ugly you think I am, and compare me to some sort of giant bird. You look so much like a bird. Dennis, I was thinking fish recently.
jokes.setup: Dee meta-commenting on the show's running gag
jokes.punchline: Mac and Dennis immediately proving her point by comparing her to animals
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-joke where Dee acknowledges the running gag and the guys immediately fall into it - sophisticated self-aware writing

jokes.text: Dennis, I was thinking fish recently. / Really? / Yeah. Her eyes are so far apart, they're like they're on...
jokes.setup: Building on the animal comparisons
jokes.punchline: Mac's fish comparison with specific anatomical observation
jokes.explanation: Escalates the animal comparison game, Mac's enthusiasm is the funny part

jokes.text: S you in your As, don't wear Cs, and J all over your Bs.
jokes.setup: Charlie attempting to say goodbye coolly
jokes.punchline: Mangling a crude phrase into incomprehensible letter combinations
jokes.explanation: Charlie's attempt to sound cool results in gibberish - perfectly captures his scrambled brain

jokes.text: Why would he not want a C...? I don't even know what he's talking about half the time, bro
jokes.setup: Charlie's incomprehensible goodbye
jokes.punchline: Mac trying to decode it and giving up in confusion
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up to Charlie's gibberish - Mac's genuine confusion sells the absurdity

jokes.text: two gorgeous girls up there, giant cans, me in the middle with my thumbs up.
jokes.setup: Frank describing his billboard vision
jokes.punchline: Crude description ending with him giving thumbs up like a perverted game show host
jokes.explanation: Frank's sleazy confidence in his terrible idea shows his complete lack of self-awareness

jokes.text: Don't flatter yourself. You're not going to be up there because I'm going to be the face of Paddy's Bar.
jokes.setup: Mac suggesting Dennis for the billboard
jokes.punchline: Dennis's immediate assumption he'll be chosen
jokes.explanation: Pure Dennis narcissism perfectly captured

jokes.text: You're... ugly. What? Ugly? I'm ugly? With that anteater nose, you're telling me I'm ugly.
jokes.setup: Dennis calling Frank ugly
jokes.punchline: Frank's shocked reaction and counter-attack on Dennis's nose
jokes.explanation: Frank's incredulous reaction to being called ugly sets up the escalating insult battle

jokes.text: My nose was chiseled by the gods themselves, Frank. My body was sculpted to the proportions of Michelangelo's David.
jokes.setup: Frank attacking Dennis's appearance
jokes.punchline: Dennis's grandiose defense comparing himself to classical art
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism - comparing himself to divine artwork with complete sincerity

jokes.text: You, on the other hand, well... you're a pit of despair. Frank, you disgust me. You disgust everyone.
jokes.setup: Dennis building up his own perfection
jokes.punchline: Dramatic turn to completely destroying Frank with theatrical language
jokes.explanation: Dennis's theatrical cruelty delivered with Shakespearean intensity - perfect character moment

jokes.text: Good morning, Philadelphia! I'm Crazy Paddy, and I'm coming to you from Paddy's Pub
jokes.setup: Dee attempting to create a viral character
jokes.punchline: Terrible over-the-top commercial persona that sounds like bad local TV
jokes.explanation: Dee's awful attempt at viral content - cringey but not exceptional compared to other moments

jokes.text: That's not what YouTube videos are like. That's like a bad late-night commercial you'd see, like, on the cable network
jokes.setup: Dee's terrible commercial-style video
jokes.punchline: Charlie accurately diagnosing exactly why it's bad
jokes.explanation: Charlie being unexpectedly insightful about media - rare moment of clarity from him

jokes.text: Charlie hits Dee in the face with volleyball during her commercial
jokes.setup: Dee trying to redo her commercial
jokes.punchline: Charlie interrupting with sudden violence for 'viral' content
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy works but relies on basic slapstick - effective but not sophisticated

jokes.text: People getting injured or seriously hurt-- that's funny!
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining why he hit Dee
jokes.punchline: His genuine belief that injury equals comedy
jokes.explanation: Charlie's twisted logic about comedy reveals his damaged worldview

jokes.text: You ever see those girls mashing the grapes, and then slips and she's, like...? / Why did you do that, Charlie? / That's what YouTube video is. That's funny?
jokes.setup: Charlie explaining viral video logic
jokes.punchline: His reference to classic physical comedy while Dee is in pain
jokes.explanation: Good meta-commentary on viral video culture

jokes.text: I can't concentrate when I'm about to get blasted in the face with a ball. I'll blast you all over if you flinch again.
jokes.setup: Dee complaining about the volleyball threat
jokes.punchline: Charlie's unintentionally sexual threat
jokes.explanation: Accidental double entendre - relies on cheap sexual humor rather than character work

jokes.text: Bro, can you believe how many hot chicks showed up for a casting call for a billboard? How have we not done this before?
jokes.setup: Mac seeing the turnout for the billboard casting
jokes.punchline: His realization that it's a great way to meet women
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac horniness disguised as business insight

jokes.text: Dennis Reynolds, 40 feet tall and shirtless. It's going to be so perfect!
jokes.setup: Dennis excited about the billboard opportunity
jokes.punchline: His fantasy of himself as a giant shirtless monument
jokes.explanation: Dennis's megalomaniacal vision of himself as public art perfectly captures his narcissism

jokes.text: I went on a stud hunt. I came back with beefcake.
jokes.setup: Frank explaining why he brought male models
jokes.punchline: His proud announcement using hunting metaphors for objectifying men
jokes.explanation: Frank's crude enthusiasm delivered with hunter's pride - good economy and character voice

jokes.text: I don't think he has a point. I'm a stallion.
jokes.setup: Discussion about whether Dennis is still attractive
jokes.punchline: Dennis's simple, confident assertion of his animal magnetism
jokes.explanation: Perfect Dennis delivery - complete confidence in his attractiveness stated as simple fact

jokes.text: 2003 Dennis, okay, he was Grade-A prime beefcake. No one is disputing that. He was a stallion. But 2008 Dennis is in decline.
jokes.setup: Mac evaluating Dennis's attractiveness
jokes.punchline: Clinical assessment of Dennis's physical decline like he's livestock
jokes.explanation: Brilliant specific dating of Dennis's peak attractiveness - treats him like a commodity with an expiration date

jokes.text: I bet if you pop those pants off, you're gonna find a bird that just won't quit either. I think the problem here is that your body quit. Your bird quit.
jokes.setup: Mac comparing Rex's body to Dennis's
jokes.punchline: Using 'bird' as slang while building to the devastating 'your bird quit'
jokes.explanation: Escalating 'quit' motif with sexual innuendo - the rhythm and repetition make it quotable

jokes.text: Attention, ladies, I'm gonna need all the A cups to please head for the back door.
jokes.setup: Mac conducting model auditions
jokes.punchline: Crudely eliminating women based on breast size
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's sexist objectification but fairly predictable for the character

jokes.text: Her name's Martina Martinez, and she is a streetwise Puerto Rican girl who's always quick with a sassy comeback.
jokes.setup: Dee explaining her new character
jokes.punchline: Obviously racist stereotype described with complete obliviousness
jokes.explanation: Dee's racist character is intentionally cringey to show her awfulness but the joke itself is fairly obvious

jokes.text: Youse a bunch of white boys, right? When you be in the clubs and you be dancing, why you look so stupid?
jokes.setup: Dee performing her racist character
jokes.punchline: Offensive stereotypical accent and behavior
jokes.explanation: Intentionally offensive to show Dee's obliviousness but relies on stereotype rather than clever writing

jokes.text: What's the pay? The pay, sir, is getting your face 40 feet up on a billboard. So there's no pay then?
jokes.setup: Dennis explaining the billboard as payment
jokes.punchline: Model cutting through the bullshit to identify lack of actual compensation
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment - cuts through Dennis's self-important presentation

jokes.text: Competitions like this aren't about money. They're about fame. They are about putting yourself on display to the world and saying, 'I'm good enough because I beat everyone else'
jokes.setup: Model pointing out there's no actual pay
jokes.punchline: Dennis's grandiose justification for exploitation disguised as opportunity
jokes.explanation: Dennis's narcissistic spin on exploitation as self-actualization - very in character

jokes.text: The first competition is the underwear competition. Boom!
jokes.setup: Frank announcing the first modeling challenge
jokes.punchline: Frank immediately stripping to underwear with enthusiasm
jokes.explanation: Frank's shameless enthusiasm for displaying his body - visual gag that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: You kept hitting me over and over in the same spot with a volleyball. And I was explicitly clear: no more Green Man.
jokes.setup: Dee addressing her injuries from Charlie's video attempts
jokes.punchline: Her frustration about both physical abuse and creative differences
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of the volleyball gag but fairly straightforward complaint

jokes.text: You're nothing without Green Man.
jokes.setup: Dee rejecting Green Man for her videos
jokes.punchline: Charlie's brutal assessment of Dee's talent
jokes.explanation: Perfect Charlie honesty and great economy of words

jokes.text: Hey, diary. It's me. It's Thursday, September 30th about midnight. I'm alone, of course, again. And I just had a little bit too much ice cream.
jokes.setup: Dee recording what she thinks is a private video diary
jokes.punchline: Pathetic confession about loneliness and emotional eating
jokes.explanation: Sets up the video diary concept that becomes important later - good character vulnerability

jokes.text: You get a whole bunch of them together, you make them compete over you, bing-bang-boom, soulmate.
jokes.setup: Mac explaining his Bachelor-inspired dating strategy
jokes.punchline: Reducing finding love to a simple competitive formula
jokes.explanation: Mac's shallow understanding of relationships reduced to reality TV formula

jokes.text: What the hell, bro? Is this supposed to be dirt? It smells like shit. It is shit. This is a barnyard.
jokes.setup: Dennis questioning the modeling setup
jokes.punchline: Frank matter-of-factly confirming it's actual excrement
jokes.explanation: Great escalation - Dennis's horror vs Frank's casual acceptance of literal shit

jokes.text: What the hell, bro? Is this supposed to be dirt? It smells like shit. / It is shit. This is a barnyard.
jokes.setup: Dennis questioning the realism of the barnyard
jokes.punchline: Frank's matter-of-fact confirmation it's actual manure
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff with Frank's commitment to authenticity

jokes.text: Rex, are you kidding me? Seriously, are you just gonna step right in it? I want to win, bro. Billboard.
jokes.setup: Rex willing to step in excrement for the competition
jokes.punchline: His simple motivation: 'Billboard'
jokes.explanation: Rex's commitment contrasted with Dennis's squeamishness - good economy in 'Billboard'

jokes.text: okay. This is Taiwan Tammy. She on runway and do a drag queen in nails.
jokes.setup: Dee introducing another problematic character
jokes.punchline: The broken English and offensive stereotype
jokes.explanation: Another offensive stereotype character, the cringe isn't enough to redeem it

jokes.text: Are you kidding me? That is extremely racist. / Isn't it awesome? I'm so excited about this one.
jokes.setup: Charlie pointing out the racism
jokes.punchline: Dee's enthusiastic obliviousness to the problem
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Dee's complete lack of awareness

jokes.text: This is Taiwan Tammy. She on runway and do a drag queen in nails.
jokes.setup: Dee introducing another racist character
jokes.punchline: Broken English stereotype with nonsensical description
jokes.explanation: Another offensive stereotype meant to show Dee's awfulness but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Goddamn it! I knew it! Charlie, what are you doing? I said no Green Man!
jokes.setup: Charlie's suspicious behavior
jokes.punchline: The inevitable Green Man reveal despite Dee's prohibition
jokes.explanation: Good payoff for the setup, Dee's frustration is earned

jokes.text: Yeah, you that, that girl, PatheticGirl43. I almost didn't recognize you in that getup, but I recognize that whiny voice anywhere.
jokes.setup: A fan recognizing Dee on the street
jokes.punchline: She's famous but for being pathetic, not glamorous
jokes.explanation: Perfect ironic twist - Dee gets recognition but it's for being pathetic, not successful

jokes.text: In other news, it seems like I have a rash in a place where a sexually active person should have a rash.
jokes.setup: Dee's diary entry taking a personal turn
jokes.punchline: Embarrassing overshare about STD-like symptoms
jokes.explanation: Good escalation of embarrassing diary content - euphemistic but clear

jokes.text: Oh, my God, you put that on YouTube? 80,000 hits. That's huge!
jokes.setup: Dee discovering her private diary was posted online
jokes.punchline: Charlie's excitement about the views vs Dee's horror
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Charlie's amoral excitement and Dee's violation - sets up her turn

jokes.text: Anya, so they are in fact, double Ds. That's great news. Why should I pick you over the other girls? I'm willing to do anything in order to win.
jokes.setup: Mac's 'interview' process with models
jokes.punchline: Reducing the conversation to breast size and sexual availability
jokes.explanation: Shows Mac's sleazy exploitation but fairly predictable for his character

jokes.text: I'm willing to do anything in order to win. / You're talking about banging me, right? / Maybe. / If you say yes... / Yes.
jokes.setup: Model expressing dedication to winning
jokes.punchline: Mac's immediate sexual proposition and her agreement
jokes.explanation: Classic Mac sexual opportunism, but fairly predictable

jokes.text: I feel like there's vibing happening right now. Yeah. I feel like we're completing each other's... sentences. I was gonna say sentences!
jokes.setup: Mac trying to create romantic connection with a contestant
jokes.punchline: Forced 'soulmate' moment with obvious word completion
jokes.explanation: Mac's desperate attempt to manufacture chemistry through obvious word games

jokes.text: Rex, that is a sexy jaguar. I mean, that jaguar is fierce! I've never been turned on by a jaguar before.
jokes.setup: Frank judging the barnyard modeling contest
jokes.punchline: His sexual attraction to Rex portraying a jungle cat
jokes.explanation: Frank's inappropriate sexual commentary disguised as judging - in character but disturbing

jokes.text: Dennis, your mule is shit. I'm no longer turned on by mules. You gave Rex jaguar! There aren't jaguars in barns.
jokes.setup: Dennis losing the animal competition to Rex
jokes.punchline: His logical complaint about animal assignment being ignored
jokes.explanation: Dennis's rare moment of logical complaint vs Frank's arbitrary judging - good frustration

jokes.text: Jesus Christ, Frank. I got burns! Nobody's gonna throw detergent in my eyeballs on a runway, Frank!
jokes.setup: Frank's increasingly dangerous challenges
jokes.punchline: Dennis's reasonable objection to actual bodily harm
jokes.explanation: Dennis's breaking point where even he recognizes Frank has gone too far

jokes.text: Jesus, Rex, I didn't tell you to start eating yet. That doesn't disqualify me, does it?
jokes.setup: Frank mentioning contestants have to eat cockroaches
jokes.punchline: Rex immediately eating them before being told to start
jokes.explanation: Rex's extreme commitment to winning shown through premature cockroach consumption

jokes.text: Listen, today was the worst day ever. I had an audition, and I didn't get the part, which is really, really strange for me.
jokes.setup: Dee's diary entry about her day
jokes.punchline: Her delusion that not getting parts is strange rather than normal
jokes.explanation: Good character delusion - Dee thinking rejection is unusual for her 'talent'

jokes.text: PatheticGirl43 here coming to you live with Philadelphia's next hot model Dennis Reynolds. Dennis, tell us what it's like to be so handsome.
jokes.setup: Dee embracing her pathetic internet fame
jokes.punchline: Interviewing Dennis while acknowledging her own pathetic brand
jokes.explanation: Good character growth - Dee embracing her pathetic fame rather than fighting it

jokes.text: Well, I like to think I've been this handsome since birth, possibly even earlier.
jokes.setup: Interview question about Dennis's handsomeness
jokes.punchline: His claim to pre-birth attractiveness
jokes.explanation: Peak Dennis narcissism - the logic-defying 'possibly even earlier' is brilliant and quotable

jokes.text: Rochelle, you taught me about love, respect, inner beauty. Dominique, you banged me, like, right away. Tabitha, you did the same. But most importantly, you banged each other. And you let me watch.
jokes.setup: Mac's Bachelor-style elimination speech
jokes.punchline: Escalating from romantic ideals to crude sexual acts
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bachelor parody escalation - from romantic to purely sexual with great rhythm

jokes.text: What? I had two of them. I had another one here. I tricked you. I got both of you guys.
jokes.setup: Mac appearing to choose one winner
jokes.punchline: His reveal that he's choosing both women
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection but the payoff is predictable Mac behavior

jokes.text: A top of the morning to both of you! I had two of them. I had another one here. I tricked you.
jokes.setup: Mac's dramatic elimination ceremony
jokes.punchline: Revealing he had two shamrocks and chose both contestants
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of Bachelor format - Mac's inability to choose leading to cheating solution

jokes.text: You just said that I taught you about inner beauty and respect. Yeah, but this whole thing's about banging, and you didn't bang me, so... You're a dick!
jokes.setup: Rochelle calling out Mac's contradictory reasoning
jokes.punchline: Mac's honest admission that it was always just about sex
jokes.explanation: Good payoff to Mac's Bachelor charade - his honesty about shallow motivations

jokes.text: It's a YouTube video that me, Dee and Charlie just made that's gonna get a million hits. So guess what, pal. Don't need your billboard. I'm going viral.
jokes.setup: Dennis boasting about their video success
jokes.punchline: His overconfidence about going viral contrasted with earlier failures
jokes.explanation: Dennis's typical overconfidence about his projects succeeding where others failed

jokes.text: I put it up right after you called me ugly. There's no billboard? Oh, yeah, sorry about that, Rex.
jokes.setup: Dennis learning Frank already put up the billboard
jokes.punchline: Frank casually dismissing Rex after putting him through the competition
jokes.explanation: Good ending twist - Frank's petty revenge and casual cruelty to Rex who went through everything
